main.description: A shared objective the crew works toward (TASK-84). Published as the latest-value artifact goal.<goal-id> — the current state of one objective — mirroring agent.status (a per-agent current status). Distinct from agent.status: a goal is a tracked OUTCOME many agents may move, not what one agent is doing right now. The goal.<id> artifact is the current value; goal.update messages are the observable stream of transitions. Content is opaque to the bus; this is a convention, not enforcement — see ADR-0035.

title.description: What the objective is, e.g. 'Ship v0.4.0' or 'Land the goal/status workstream'.

state.description: Coarse state, one of: pending (declared, not started) | active (in progress) | blocked (HARD blocked, needs help) | done (achieved) | dropped (abandoned). Drives the dash Goals pill.

progress.description: Optional short progress, e.g. '3/5 tickets' or '60%'. Opaque to the bus; rendered as-is.

headline.description: Optional present-tense line of the latest movement, e.g. 'PR #143 merged; tracker next'.

owner.description: Optional agent accountable for the goal (convenience label).

by.description: Who last set it (convenience label; the bus-stamped author of the artifact write is authoritative).
